title: ranunculus laetus subsp. pseudolaetus
intro: Ranunculus laetus subsp. pseudolaetus, commonly known as the lesser celandine or fig buttercup, is a plant species in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids. The health benefits of Ranunculus laetus subsp. pseudolaetus include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for wound healing and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
